title: Lab 24: Password Aging Defaults
description: Configure stronger new-user aging defaults, including inactive days.

tasks: Configure the system defaults for newly created local users so that the maximum password age is 60 days, the minimum age is 7 days, and the warning period is 10 days.
Configure the default inactive period for newly created local users to 15 days.
Create the user drift24, set its password to cinder9, and ensure the user inherits the default password aging policy.
hints: PASS_MAX_DAYS, PASS_MIN_DAYS, and PASS_WARN_AGE live in /etc/login.defs.
Use useradd -D to set and verify the inactive default.
checks: getent passwd drift24 >/dev/null && chage -l drift24 | grep -Eq '^Maximum number of days between password change[[:space:]]*:[[:space:]]*60$' && chage -l drift24 | grep -Eq '^Minimum number of days between password change[[:space:]]*:[[:space:]]*7$' && chage -l drift24 | grep -Eq '^Number of days of warning before password expires[[:space:]]*:[[:space:]]*10$'
solution_outline: Update login.defs with the requested values.
Create drift24, set the password, and verify the inherited policy.
task_titles: Set password aging defaults in login.defs
Create drift24 with the inherited defaults
